evals.prompt: I want to create a skill for specifically handling Git merge conflicts. It should explain the markers and help the user pick which side to keep.
evals.expected_output: Initial folder structure for 'git-conflict-resolver'. Draft of SKILL.md with imperative instructions on markers (<<<<<<<, =======, >>>>>>>).
assertions: The draft SKILL.md includes a description starting with 'Use this skill to...'
The model suggests iterative evaluation as a step

evals.prompt: My 'git-conflict-resolver' skill isn't triggering when I say 'help me fix this merge'. How do I fix the description?
evals.expected_output: Advice to make the description more 'pushy' and inclusive of user intent. Suggestion like: 'Use this skill WHENEVER the user encounters Git conflict markers or needs help resolving merges.'
assertions: The model identifies that the current description might be too narrow
The recommendation follows the 'imperative' and 'user intent' principles
The model warns against 'overfitting' to the specific prompt
